div1.entryFree:
Reform, vb. 1) to change from worse to better: they are --ed, civil, full of good, Gent. V, 4, 156. thy penitent --ed, Wint. I, 2, 239. what you would have --ed, John IV, 2, 44. as we hear you do r. yourselves, H4B V, 5, 72.
2) to abolish, to redress, to remedy: takes on him to r. some certain edicts, H4A IV, 3, 78. which are heresies, and, not --ed, may prove pernicious, H8 V, 3, 19. I hope we have --ed that indifferently with us, sir. O, r. it altogether, Hml. III, 2, 40. Hml. III, 2, 40
Misapplied for to inform by Dogberry: Ado V, 1, 262.
